title: Conjecture 1.1 (Drier–Linial)
statement_text: For $\ell\geq\Omega(n)$ almost all $\ell$-lifts of $K_{n}$ have Hajós number equal to $\Theta(n)$.
context_text: The problem of determining the Hajós number (size of the largest topological clique) in random lifts was first raised by Drier and Linial [16] in 2006. They showed that almost every $\ell$-lift of $K_n$ contains a topological clique of order at least $(1-o(1))\frac{\ell}{3}$ when $\ell < (1-o(1))\frac{n}{2}$, which together with the trivial upper bound of $n$ established the $\Theta(n)$ order for that range of $\ell$, but left the full range open. Witkowski [42] later verified the conjecture for $\ell$ exponential in $n$.
paper_contribution: Confirms the conjecture in a strong form: for $\ell \geq (1+o(1))n$ one can almost surely find a subdivision of $K_n$ itself (Hajós number exactly $n$), and the result is tight since for $\ell \leq (1-o(1))n$ almost all $\ell$-lifts do not contain a subdivision of $K_n$.
